item.title: [7] Spin-liquid-like spin dynamics in the frustrated antiferromagnet TbBO3
item.description:
Last authors: D. T. Adroja, P. Khuntia

Authors: J. Khatua, D. Tay, T. Shiroka, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant study of a quantum frustrated magnet.

Tags: #spin-liquid #antiferromagnet

Abstract: Author(s): J. Khatua, D. Tay, T. Shiroka, M. Pregelj, K. Kargeti, S. K. Panda, G. B. G. Stenning, P. Manuel, M. D. Le, D. T. Adroja, and P. Khuntia The synergistic interplay between spin correlations, spin-orbit coupling, and competing exchange interactions provides a promising route to realize exotic quantum states with nontrivial excitations in rare-earth-based frustrated magnets. Here, by using thermodynamic and local-probe measurements down… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174406] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): J. Khatua, D. Tay, T. Shiroka, M. Pregelj, K. Kargeti, S. K. Panda, G. B. G. Stenning, P. Manuel, M. D. Le, D. T. Adroja, and P. Khuntia

The synergistic interplay between spin correlations, spin-orbit coupling, and competing exchange interactions provides a promising route to realize exotic quantum states with nontrivial excitations in rare-earth-based frustrated magnets. Here, by using thermodynamic and local-probe measurements down…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174406] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Spin-liquid-like spin dynamics in the frustrated antiferromagnet TbBO3
item.dc:creator: J. Khatua, D. Tay, T. Shiroka, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [8] 3Q magnetic order with spatially alternating spin scalar chirality in overdoped Co0.336TaS2
item.description:
Last authors: Scott A. Crooker, Je-Geun Park

Authors: Woonghee Cho, Pyeongjae Park, Chaebin Kim, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Important experimental study on topological magnetic order.

Tags: #neutron-scattering #topological #Hall-effect

Abstract: Author(s): Woonghee Cho, Pyeongjae Park, Chaebin Kim, Yeochan An, Kazuki Iida, Ryoichi Kajimoto, Sakib Matin, Romain Sibille, Scott A. Crooker, and Je-Geun Park While underdoped Co 1 / 3 TaS 2 shows a topological Hall effect, the overdoped Co 0 . 336 TaS 2 variant does not. Traditionally, this was attributed to a simple helical spin order. However, using multimodal techniques—including inelastic neutron scattering and optical dichroism—the authors propose here a more complex triple-Q order. In this state, the spin scalar chirality alternates spatially, effectively cancelling the macroscopic Hall response. This study provides a new framework for detecting “hidden” multi-Q spin textures that standard transport measurements might miss. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174410] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Woonghee Cho, Pyeongjae Park, Chaebin Kim, Yeochan An, Kazuki Iida, Ryoichi Kajimoto, Sakib Matin, Romain Sibille, Scott A. Crooker, and Je-Geun Park

While underdoped Co1/3TaS2 shows a topological Hall effect, the overdoped Co0.336TaS2 variant does not. Traditionally, this was attributed to a simple helical spin order. However, using multimodal techniques—including inelastic neutron scattering and optical dichroism—the authors propose here a more complex triple-Q order. In this state, the spin scalar chirality alternates spatially, effectively cancelling the macroscopic Hall response. This study provides a new framework for detecting “hidden” multi-Q spin textures that standard transport measurements might miss.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174410] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] 3Q magnetic order with spatially alternating spin scalar chirality in overdoped Co0.336TaS2
item.dc:creator: Woonghee Cho, Pyeongjae Park, Chaebin Kim, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [8] Dependence of superconducting and pseudogap quasiparticle dynamics on the number of CuO2 planes in Bi-based cuprate superconductors
item.description:
Last authors: Hiroshi Eisaki, Migaku Oda

Authors: Taisei Shimizu, Tohru Kurosawa, Satoshi Tsuchiya, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Important experiment on high-Tc cuprate physics.

Tags: #cuprate #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Taisei Shimizu, Tohru Kurosawa, Satoshi Tsuchiya, Yasunori Toda, Shigeyuki Ishida, Hiroshi Eisaki, and Migaku Oda The maximum superconducting transition temperature ${T}_{c,\mathrm{max}}$ in cuprate superconductors exhibits a strong dependence on the number of ${\mathrm{CuO}}_{2}$ planes $n$ per structural cell, exceeding 100 K in trilayer compounds ($n=3$). Elucidating the mechanism responsible for this enhanc… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174510] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Taisei Shimizu, Tohru Kurosawa, Satoshi Tsuchiya, Yasunori Toda, Shigeyuki Ishida, Hiroshi Eisaki, and Migaku Oda

The maximum superconducting transition temperature Tc,max in cuprate superconductors exhibits a strong dependence on the number of CuO2 planes n per structural cell, exceeding 100 K in trilayer compounds (n=3). Elucidating the mechanism responsible for this enhancement of superconductivity in trilay…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174510] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Dependence of superconducting and pseudogap quasiparticle dynamics on the number of CuO2 planes in Bi-based cuprate superconductors
item.dc:creator: Taisei Shimizu, Tohru Kurosawa, Satoshi Tsuchiya, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [8] Evolution of superconductivity evidence in pressurized La3−xSmxNi2O7
item.description:
Last authors: Hualei Sun, Meng Wang

Authors: Qingyi Zhong, Junfeng Chen, Zhengyang Qiu, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study on nickelate superconductors.

Tags: #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Qingyi Zhong, Junfeng Chen, Zhengyang Qiu, Jingyuan Li, Xing Huang, Peiyue Ma, Mengwu Huo, Hongliang Dong, Sihao Deng, Lunhua He, Yifeng Han, Hualei Sun, and Meng Wang Motivated by the discovery of superconductivity in bilayer ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$ at 80 K and the increased superconducting transition temperature, ${T}_{\text{c}}$, up to 96 K in single crystals of ${\mathrm{La}}_{1.57}{\mathrm{Sm}}_{1.43}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174512] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qingyi Zhong, Junfeng Chen, Zhengyang Qiu, Jingyuan Li, Xing Huang, Peiyue Ma, Mengwu Huo, Hongliang Dong, Sihao Deng, Lunhua He, Yifeng Han, Hualei Sun, and Meng Wang

Motivated by the discovery of superconductivity in bilayer La3Ni2O7 at 80 K and the increased superconducting transition temperature, Tc, up to 96 K in single crystals of La1.57Sm1.43Ni2O7−δ under pressure, we systematically study the effect of Sm substitution on the superconductivity and structure …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174512] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Evolution of superconductivity evidence in pressurized La3−xSmxNi2O7
item.dc:creator: Qingyi Zhong, Junfeng Chen, Zhengyang Qiu, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: [8] Pressure-induced electronic band evolution and observation of superconductivity in the Dirac semimetal ZrTe5
item.description:
Last authors: Jinguang Cheng, Prashant Shahi

Authors: Sanskar Mishra, Nagendra Singh, Vinod K. Gangwar, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experiment on topological semimetals under pressure.

Tags: #Dirac #topological #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Sanskar Mishra, Nagendra Singh, Vinod K. Gangwar, Rajan Walia, Jianping Sun, Genfu Chen, Dilip Bhoi, Sandip Chatterjee, Yoshiya Uwatoko, Jinguang Cheng, and Prashant Shahi We report a comprehensive investigation of the pressure effects on the magnetotransport properties of the topological material ${\mathrm{ZrTe}}_{5}$ within 1–8 GPa pressure range. With increasing pressure, the characteristic peak (${T}_{p}$) in its electrical resistivity $ρ(T)$ first shifts to highe… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195113] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sanskar Mishra, Nagendra Singh, Vinod K. Gangwar, Rajan Walia, Jianping Sun, Genfu Chen, Dilip Bhoi, Sandip Chatterjee, Yoshiya Uwatoko, Jinguang Cheng, and Prashant Shahi

We report a comprehensive investigation of the pressure effects on the magnetotransport properties of the topological material ZrTe5 within 1–8 GPa pressure range. With increasing pressure, the characteristic peak (Tp) in its electrical resistivity ρ(T) first shifts to higher temperature and then mo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195113] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Pressure-induced electronic band evolution and observation of superconductivity in the Dirac semimetal ZrTe5
item.dc:creator: Sanskar Mishra, Nagendra Singh, Vinod K. Gangwar, et al.

item.title: [7] Interfacial bond tailored altermagnetic tunnel junctions
item.description:
Last authors: Jinbo Yang, Jie Yang

Authors: Chao Mao, Shiqi Liu, Shunfang Li, Jinbo Yang, Jie Yang

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant to altermagnetism and magnetic tunnel junctions.

Tags: #altermagnet #ferromagnet #antiferromagnet

Abstract: Author(s): Chao Mao, Shiqi Liu, Shunfang Li, Jinbo Yang, and Jie Yang Altermagnets, a distinctive class of unconventional antiferromagnets featuring compensated magnetization but ferromagnetlike spin splitting, have attracted significant interest as promising candidates for next-generation memories. Here, we design an altermagnetic tunnel junction (AMTJ) based on the … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174409] Published Wed May 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chao Mao, Shiqi Liu, Shunfang Li, Jinbo Yang, and Jie Yang

Altermagnets, a distinctive class of unconventional antiferromagnets featuring compensated magnetization but ferromagnetlike spin splitting, have attracted significant interest as promising candidates for next-generation memories. Here, we design an altermagnetic tunnel junction (AMTJ) based on the …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174409] Published Wed May 13, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Interfacial bond tailored altermagnetic tunnel junctions
item.dc:creator: Chao Mao, Shiqi Liu, Shunfang Li, Jinbo Yang, Jie Yang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [7] High-efficiency superconducting diode effect in a gate-tunable double-loop SQUID
item.description:
Last authors: Jukka I. Väyrynen, Michael J. Manfra

Authors: Wyatt Gibbons, Teng Zhang, Kevin Barrow, Tyler Lindemann, Jukka I. Väyrynen, Michael J. Manfra

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant experimental study of superconducting diode effects in hybrid junctions.

Tags: #superconduct

Abstract: Author(s): Wyatt Gibbons, Teng Zhang, Kevin Barrow, Tyler Lindemann, Jukka I. Väyrynen, and Michael J. Manfra The authors present here measurements of a double-loop superconducting quantum interference device (SQUID) with two gate-tunable hybrid InAs/Al Josephson junctions on each branch. This device enables controlled interferometry of three highly non-sinusoidal current-phase relationships (CPRs), revealing a significant superconducting diode effect. Through optimized gate and flux tuning, they achieve a device configuration where more than triple the supercurrent can flow in one direction compared to the other, and they accurately model this behavior with the expected CPR of the full device. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174505] Published Wed May 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wyatt Gibbons, Teng Zhang, Kevin Barrow, Tyler Lindemann, Jukka I. Väyrynen, and Michael J. Manfra

The authors present here measurements of a double-loop superconducting quantum interference device (SQUID) with two gate-tunable hybrid InAs/Al Josephson junctions on each branch. This device enables controlled interferometry of three highly non-sinusoidal current-phase relationships (CPRs), revealing a significant superconducting diode effect. Through optimized gate and flux tuning, they achieve a device configuration where more than triple the supercurrent can flow in one direction compared to the other, and they accurately model this behavior with the expected CPR of the full device.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174505] Published Wed May 13, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] High-efficiency superconducting diode effect in a gate-tunable double-loop SQUID
item.dc:creator: Wyatt Gibbons, Teng Zhang, Kevin Barrow, Tyler Lindemann, Jukka I. Väyrynen, Michael J. Manfra
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [8] Doping evolution of the charge density wave and charge density fluctuations in La2−xSrxCuO4
item.description:
Last authors: Ke-Jin Zhou, Stephen M. Hayden

Authors: Charles C. Tam, Mengze Zhu, Maud C. Barthélemy, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Important study on CDW dynamics in cuprates.

Tags: #chargedensitywave #CDW #cuprate #superconduct

Abstract: Author(s): Charles C. Tam, Mengze Zhu, Maud C. Barthélemy, Lauren J. Cane, Oliver J. Lipscombe, Stefano Agrestini, Jaewon Choi, Mirian Garcia-Fernandez, Ke-Jin Zhou, and Stephen M. Hayden Cuprate superconductors show various collective charge correlations that are intimately connected with their electronic properties. In particular, charge order in the form of an incommensurate charge density wave (CDW) order with an in-plane wave vector ${δ}_{\text{CDW}}≈$ 0.23–0.35 reciprocal latti… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174506] Published Wed May 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Charles C. Tam, Mengze Zhu, Maud C. Barthélemy, Lauren J. Cane, Oliver J. Lipscombe, Stefano Agrestini, Jaewon Choi, Mirian Garcia-Fernandez, Ke-Jin Zhou, and Stephen M. Hayden

Cuprate superconductors show various collective charge correlations that are intimately connected with their electronic properties. In particular, charge order in the form of an incommensurate charge density wave (CDW) order with an in-plane wave vector δCDW≈ 0.23–0.35 reciprocal lattice units appea…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174506] Published Wed May 13, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Doping evolution of the charge density wave and charge density fluctuations in La2−xSrxCuO4
item.dc:creator: Charles C. Tam, Mengze Zhu, Maud C. Barthélemy, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: [7] Chiral superconductivity from a parent Chern band and its non-Abelian generalization
item.description:
Last authors: Zhi-Qiang Gao, Hui Yang

Authors: Yan-Qi Wang, Zhi-Qiang Gao, Hui Yang

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical investigation of superconductivity in Chern bands/graphene.

Tags: #Chern #graphene #superconduct

Abstract: Author(s): Yan-Qi Wang, Zhi-Qiang Gao, and Hui Yang We propose a minimal model starting from a parent Chern band with quartic dispersion that can describe the spin-valley polarized electrons in rhombohedral tetralayer graphene. The interplay between repulsive and attractive interactions on top of that parent Chern band is studied. We conduct standard… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174507] Published Wed May 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yan-Qi Wang, Zhi-Qiang Gao, and Hui Yang

We propose a minimal model starting from a parent Chern band with quartic dispersion that can describe the spin-valley polarized electrons in rhombohedral tetralayer graphene. The interplay between repulsive and attractive interactions on top of that parent Chern band is studied. We conduct standard…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174507] Published Wed May 13, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Chiral superconductivity from a parent Chern band and its non-Abelian generalization
item.dc:creator: Yan-Qi Wang, Zhi-Qiang Gao, Hui Yang

item.title: [9] Interplay of magnetic and thermodynamic responses in the kagome triangular system R3Sb3A2O14 (R=rare earth; A=Mg, Zn)
item.description:
Last authors: Bing Huang, Tianxing Ma

Authors: Zixuan Jia, Lufeng Zhang, Qingzhuo Duan, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of kagome lattice physics, highly relevant to research interests.

Tags: #kagome #Hubbard

Abstract: Author(s): Zixuan Jia, Lufeng Zhang, Qingzhuo Duan, Zenghui Fan, Jingyao Wang, Ying Liang, Bing Huang, and Tianxing Ma Inspired by the recent experimental progress in pyrochlore derivative ${\mathit{R}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{3}{\mathit{A}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{14}$ $(R=\text{rare earth};$ $A=\mathrm{Mg}, \mathrm{Zn})$, we investigate the Hubbard model on the kagome lattice with an additional hopping ${t}^{′}/t$, which enabl… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195109] Published Wed May 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zixuan Jia, Lufeng Zhang, Qingzhuo Duan, Zenghui Fan, Jingyao Wang, Ying Liang, Bing Huang, and Tianxing Ma

Inspired by the recent experimental progress in pyrochlore derivative R3Sb3A2O14 (R=rare earth; A=Mg, Zn), we investigate the Hubbard model on the kagome lattice with an additional hopping t′/t, which enables continuous interpolation between the kagome and triangular lattices by using determinant qu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195109] Published Wed May 13, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Interplay of magnetic and thermodynamic responses in the kagome triangular system R3Sb3A2O14 (R=rare earth; A=Mg, Zn)
item.dc:creator: Zixuan Jia, Lufeng Zhang, Qingzhuo Duan, et al.

item.title: [8] Phonons drive the topological phase transition in quasi-one-dimensional Bi4I4
item.description:
Last authors: Jin-Jian Zhou, Yugui Yao

Authors: Wenjie Hu, Jiayi Gong, Yuhui Qiu, Lexian Yang, Jin-Jian Zhou, Yugui Yao

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental-theory link on a quasi-1D topological material.

Tags: #topological #phonon #quasi-1D

Abstract: Author(s): Wenjie Hu, Jiayi Gong, Yuhui Qiu, Lexian Yang, Jin-Jian Zhou, and Yugui Yao Quasi-one-dimensional bismuth halides offer an exceptional platform for exploring diverse topological phases, yet the nature of the room-temperature topological phase transition in ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{4}{\mathrm{I}}_{4}$ remains unresolved. While theory predicts the high-temperature $β$ phase to be a st… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L201111] Published Wed May 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wenjie Hu, Jiayi Gong, Yuhui Qiu, Lexian Yang, Jin-Jian Zhou, and Yugui Yao

Quasi-one-dimensional bismuth halides offer an exceptional platform for exploring diverse topological phases, yet the nature of the room-temperature topological phase transition in Bi4I4 remains unresolved. While theory predicts the high-temperature β phase to be a strong topological insulator (TI),…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L201111] Published Wed May 13, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Phonons drive the topological phase transition in quasi-one-dimensional Bi4I4

item.title: [8] Tian-Ye-Jin scaling analysis of the anomalous Hall effect and strong electron-magnon scattering in the van der Waals ferromagnet Fe3GaTe2
item.description:
Last authors: Jun Hu, Baomin Wang

Authors: Lanfeng Liu, Yitao Zheng, Hongyuan Wang, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of AHE and electronic scattering in a magnetic vdW material.

Tags: #anomalousHall #vanderWaals #ferromagnet

Abstract: Author(s): Lanfeng Liu, Yitao Zheng, Hongyuan Wang, Bangyong Yan, Mengting Zou, Wei Li, Jun Hu, and Baomin Wang The significance of anomalous Hall effect (AHE) lies in its capability of the interconversion between spin and charge information. The van der Waals (vdW) ferromagnet $\mathrm{F}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{GaT}{\mathrm{e}}_{2}$ has attracted extensive interest owing to its high Curie temperature and str… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184432] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lanfeng Liu, Yitao Zheng, Hongyuan Wang, Bangyong Yan, Mengting Zou, Wei Li, Jun Hu, and Baomin Wang

The significance of anomalous Hall effect (AHE) lies in its capability of the interconversion between spin and charge information. The van der Waals (vdW) ferromagnet Fe3GaTe2 has attracted extensive interest owing to its high Curie temperature and strong perpendicular magnetic anisotropy. Here, we …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184432] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Tian-Ye-Jin scaling analysis of the anomalous Hall effect and strong electron-magnon scattering in the van der Waals ferromagnet Fe3GaTe2

item.title: [8] Spin-valley splitting in altermagnet/ferromagnet Ca(CoN)2/CoX (X=Cl2,Se) heterostructures
item.description:
Last authors: Chenqiang Hua, Fei Song

Authors: Zhaofeng Liang, Jinsen Zhang, Yao Wang, Chenqiang Hua, Fei Song

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant study on altermagnets and spin-valley physics in heterostructures.

Tags: #altermagnet #heterostructure

Abstract: Author(s): Zhaofeng Liang, Jinsen Zhang, Yao Wang, Chenqiang Hua, and Fei Song Altermagnets with spin-layer coupling (SLC) enable symmetry-protected spin splitting without net magnetization. In the SLC altermagnet $\mathrm{Ca}{(\mathrm{CoN})}_{2}$ monolayer, which exhibits distinct sublayer-dependent spin and valley characteristics, we demonstrate that breaking the sublayer-ex… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184434] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhaofeng Liang, Jinsen Zhang, Yao Wang, Chenqiang Hua, and Fei Song

Altermagnets with spin-layer coupling (SLC) enable symmetry-protected spin splitting without net magnetization. In the SLC altermagnet Ca(CoN)2 monolayer, which exhibits distinct sublayer-dependent spin and valley characteristics, we demonstrate that breaking the sublayer-exchange symmetry S via fer…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184434] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Spin-valley splitting in altermagnet/ferromagnet Ca(CoN)2/CoX (X=Cl2,Se) heterostructures

item.title: [7] Dynamics and thermodynamics of the S=52 almost Heisenberg triangular lattice antiferromagnet K2Mn(SeO3)2
item.description:
Last authors: Z. Yan, S. Gvasaliya, A. L. Chernyshev, and A. Zheludev

Authors: Mengze Zhu, V. Romerio, D. Moser, K. Yu. Povarov, R. Sibille, R. Wawrzyńczak, Z. Yan, S. Gvasaliya, A. L. Chernyshev, and A. Zheludev

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Neutron scattering study on a frustrated magnetic lattice.

Tags: #neutronscattering #antiferromagnet

Abstract: Author(s): Mengze Zhu, V. Romerio, D. Moser, K. Yu. Povarov, R. Sibille, R. Wawrzyńczak, Z. Yan, S. Gvasaliya, A. L. Chernyshev, and A. Zheludev We report calorimetric, magnetic, and neutron scattering studies on an $S=5/2$, nearly Heisenberg triangular-lattice antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{K}}_{2}\mathrm{Mn}{({\mathrm{SeO}}_{3})}_{2}$ with weak XXZ easy-axis anisotropy. Multiple magnetic phases are identified, including a noncollinear Y phase i… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184436] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mengze Zhu, V. Romerio, D. Moser, K. Yu. Povarov, R. Sibille, R. Wawrzyńczak, Z. Yan, S. Gvasaliya, A. L. Chernyshev, and A. Zheludev

We report calorimetric, magnetic, and neutron scattering studies on an S=5/2, nearly Heisenberg triangular-lattice antiferromagnet K2Mn(SeO3)2 with weak XXZ easy-axis anisotropy. Multiple magnetic phases are identified, including a noncollinear Y phase in zero field, a field-induced collinear m=1/3 …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184436] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Dynamics and thermodynamics of the S=52 almost Heisenberg triangular lattice antiferromagnet K2Mn(SeO3)2
item.dc:creator: Mengze Zhu, V. Romerio, D. Moser, K. Yu. Povarov, R. Sibille, R. Wawrzyńczak, Z. Yan, S. Gvasaliya, A. L. Chernyshev, and A. Zheludev

item.title: [7] Contrasting g-factor anisotropy in easy-plane and easy-axis van der Waals CrX3 (X=Cl, Br, I) magnetic materials
item.description:
Last authors: Yunhui Li, Yizheng Wu

Authors: Nan Jiang, Hongyue Xu, Haoran Chen, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental investigation of vdW magnets pertinent to magnetic topological interests.

Tags: #vanderWaals #magnetic

Abstract: Author(s): Nan Jiang, Hongyue Xu, Haoran Chen, Yuanfei Fan, Tong Wu, Zhen Cheng, Yizi Feng, Yunhui Li, and Yizheng Wu We systematically investigate $g$-factor anisotropy in two-dimensional van der Waals magnets $\mathrm{Cr}{X}_{3}$ $(X=\mathrm{Cl}, \mathrm{Br}, \mathrm{I})$ using broadband magnetic resonance for magnetic ordered states below the critical temperature $({T}_{\text{cr}})$ and for paramagnetic states a… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184437] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nan Jiang, Hongyue Xu, Haoran Chen, Yuanfei Fan, Tong Wu, Zhen Cheng, Yizi Feng, Yunhui Li, and Yizheng Wu

We systematically investigate g-factor anisotropy in two-dimensional van der Waals magnets CrX3 (X=Cl, Br, I) using broadband magnetic resonance for magnetic ordered states below the critical temperature (Tcr) and for paramagnetic states above Tcr, with varied field orientations. Despite sharing the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184437] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Contrasting g-factor anisotropy in easy-plane and easy-axis van der Waals CrX3 (X=Cl, Br, I) magnetic materials
item.dc:creator: Nan Jiang, Hongyue Xu, Haoran Chen, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [9] Double-Q chiral stripe order in the anomalous Hall antiferromagnet CoNb3S6
item.description:
Last authors: Cristian D. Batista, Kemp W. Plumb

Authors: Ben Zager, Shang-Shun Zhang, Hana Schiff, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct relevance to anomalous Hall antiferromagnets and magnetic order.

Tags: #anomalousHall #antiferromagnet

Abstract: Author(s): Ben Zager, Shang-Shun Zhang, Hana Schiff, Raymond Fan, Paul Steadman, Cristian D. Batista, and Kemp W. Plumb We present fine momentum space resolution resonant elastic x-ray scattering measurements of the magnetic structure of the metallic antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{CoNb}}_{3}{\mathrm{S}}_{6}$. Using circular dichroism and full linear polarization analysis of the magnetic scattering, we reveal a noncoplanar… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184441] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ben Zager, Shang-Shun Zhang, Hana Schiff, Raymond Fan, Paul Steadman, Cristian D. Batista, and Kemp W. Plumb

We present fine momentum space resolution resonant elastic x-ray scattering measurements of the magnetic structure of the metallic antiferromagnet CoNb3S6. Using circular dichroism and full linear polarization analysis of the magnetic scattering, we reveal a noncoplanar double-Q (2Q) order that is c…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184441] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Double-Q chiral stripe order in the anomalous Hall antiferromagnet CoNb3S6
item.dc:creator: Ben Zager, Shang-Shun Zhang, Hana Schiff, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Anomalous phase shift and superconducting diode effect in Josephson junctions via thin films of rare-earth intermetallic magnets
item.description:
Last authors: I. V. Bobkova, A. M. Bobkov, S. V. Eremeev, and E. V. Chulkov

Authors: G. A. Bobkov, I. A. Shvets, I. V. Bobkova, A. M. Bobkov, S. V. Eremeev, and E. V. Chulkov

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Superconducting diode effect in S/F/S junctions, relevant to magnetic topological materials.

Tags: #superconduct #ferromagnet

Abstract: Author(s): G. A. Bobkov, I. A. Shvets, I. V. Bobkova, A. M. Bobkov, S. V. Eremeev, and E. V. Chulkov The superconductor/ferromagnet/superconductor (S/F/S) Josephson junctions (JJs) with an anomalous ground state phase shift ${φ}_{0}≠0,π$ $({φ}_{0}\text{−}\mathrm{S}/\mathrm{F}/\mathrm{S} \mathrm{JJs})$ enable the implementation of the zero-field Josephson diode effect with the possibility to control… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184516] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): G. A. Bobkov, I. A. Shvets, I. V. Bobkova, A. M. Bobkov, S. V. Eremeev, and E. V. Chulkov

The superconductor/ferromagnet/superconductor (S/F/S) Josephson junctions (JJs) with an anomalous ground state phase shift φ0≠0,π (φ0−S/F/S JJs) enable the implementation of the zero-field Josephson diode effect with the possibility to control the diode efficiency and polarity. It is just as importa…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184516] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Anomalous phase shift and superconducting diode effect in Josephson junctions via thin films of rare-earth intermetallic magnets
item.dc:creator: G. A. Bobkov, I. A. Shvets, I. V. Bobkova, A. M. Bobkov, S. V. Eremeev, and E. V. Chulkov
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [8] Twisted bilayer graphene from first principles: Structural and electronic properties
item.description:
Last authors: Efstratios Manousakis, Efthimios Kaxiras

Authors: Albert Zhu, Daniel Bennett, Daniel T. Larson, Mohammed M. Al Ezzi, Efstratios Manousakis, Efthimios Kaxiras

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: DFT study on twisted bilayer graphene, well within the user's broader interest range.

Tags: #graphene #twistedbilayer

Abstract: Author(s): Albert Zhu, Daniel Bennett, Daniel T. Larson, Mohammed M. Al Ezzi, Efstratios Manousakis, and Efthimios Kaxiras We present a comprehensive first-principles study of twisted bilayer graphene (tBLG) for a wide range of twist angles, with a focus on structural and electronic properties. By employing density functional theory (DFT) with an optimized local basis set, we simulate tBLG, obtaining fully relaxed comme… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205125] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Albert Zhu, Daniel Bennett, Daniel T. Larson, Mohammed M. Al Ezzi, Efstratios Manousakis, and Efthimios Kaxiras

We present a comprehensive first-principles study of twisted bilayer graphene (tBLG) for a wide range of twist angles, with a focus on structural and electronic properties. By employing density functional theory (DFT) with an optimized local basis set, we simulate tBLG, obtaining fully relaxed comme…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205125] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Twisted bilayer graphene from first principles: Structural and electronic properties
item.dc:creator: Albert Zhu, Daniel Bennett, Daniel T. Larson, Mohammed M. Al Ezzi, Efstratios Manousakis, Efthimios Kaxiras

item.title: [9] Carrier-density dependence of magnetotransport in the correlated Dirac semimetal CaIrO3
item.description:
Last authors: Daisuke Hashizume, Yoshinori Tokura

Authors: Rinsuke Yamada, Jun Fujioka, Minoru Kawamura, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct relevance to Dirac semimetals and correlated electronic systems.

Tags: #Dirac #correlated

Abstract: Author(s): Rinsuke Yamada, Jun Fujioka, Minoru Kawamura, Tatsuya Okawa, Yoshio Kaneko, Shiro Sakai, Motoaki Hirayama, Ryotaro Arita, Kiyohiro Adachi, Daisuke Hashizume, and Yoshinori Tokura We report the carrier-density dependence of the magnetotransport property in the correlated Dirac semimetal ${\mathrm{CaIrO}}_{3}$. In the dilute carrier density region (${n}_{\mathrm{H}} ∼2.2×{10}^{16}\phantom{\rule{0.16em}{0ex}}{\mathrm{cm}}^{−3}$) at $2\phantom{\rule{0.16em}{0ex}}\mathrm{K}$, the… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205126] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rinsuke Yamada, Jun Fujioka, Minoru Kawamura, Tatsuya Okawa, Yoshio Kaneko, Shiro Sakai, Motoaki Hirayama, Ryotaro Arita, Kiyohiro Adachi, Daisuke Hashizume, and Yoshinori Tokura

We report the carrier-density dependence of the magnetotransport property in the correlated Dirac semimetal CaIrO3. In the dilute carrier density region (nH ∼2.2×1016cm−3) at 2K, the mobility exceeds 1.0×105cm2/Vs at 2K, and the transverse magnetoresistance reaches 2000% at 12T. The analysis of quan…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205126] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Carrier-density dependence of magnetotransport in the correlated Dirac semimetal CaIrO3
item.dc:creator: Rinsuke Yamada, Jun Fujioka, Minoru Kawamura, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: [7] Dichroism from chiral thermoelectric probes: Generalized sum rules for orbital and heat magnetizations
item.description:
Last authors: Anaïs Defossez, Nathan Goldman

Authors: Baptiste Bermond, Lucila Peralta Gavensky, Anaïs Defossez, Nathan Goldman

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical framework connecting dichroism to topological responses and magnetization.

Tags: #Chern #topological

Abstract: Author(s): Baptiste Bermond, Lucila Peralta Gavensky, Anaïs Defossez, and Nathan Goldman The authors introduce here a unified sum‑rule framework linking thermoelectric excitation spectra to ground‑state properties and encompassing both topological responses and magnetizations. Under chiral thermoelectric driving, dichroic absorption directly probes Kubo correlators, placing orbital and heat magnetizations on equal footing with the Chern number. A hierarchical decomposition, real‑space markers, and a heat quantum metric emerge, opening practical routes to measuring thermoelectric responses in quantum matter. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205127] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Baptiste Bermond, Lucila Peralta Gavensky, Anaïs Defossez, and Nathan Goldman

The authors introduce here a unified sum‑rule framework linking thermoelectric excitation spectra to ground‑state properties and encompassing both topological responses and magnetizations. Under chiral thermoelectric driving, dichroic absorption directly probes Kubo correlators, placing orbital and heat magnetizations on equal footing with the Chern number. A hierarchical decomposition, real‑space markers, and a heat quantum metric emerge, opening practical routes to measuring thermoelectric responses in quantum matter.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205127] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Dichroism from chiral thermoelectric probes: Generalized sum rules for orbital and heat magnetizations
item.dc:creator: Baptiste Bermond, Lucila Peralta Gavensky, Anaïs Defossez, Nathan Goldman
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: [8] Magnetically tunable quantum Hall triple-antidot molecules realized in suspended graphene
item.description:
Last authors: Dmitri V. Averin, Xu Du

Authors: Naomi Mizuno, Dmitri V. Averin, Xu Du

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental quantum Hall study in graphene.

Tags: #graphene #quantumHall

Abstract: Author(s): Naomi Mizuno, Dmitri V. Averin, and Xu Du We report the realization and modeling of a triple-antidot molecule hosting three interacting quantum Hall quasiparticles, with tunnel coupling between antidots tunable via the magnetic field. The measured tunneling conductance spectrum reveals the molecular energy levels arising from the interantid… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205128] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Naomi Mizuno, Dmitri V. Averin, and Xu Du

We report the realization and modeling of a triple-antidot molecule hosting three interacting quantum Hall quasiparticles, with tunnel coupling between antidots tunable via the magnetic field. The measured tunneling conductance spectrum reveals the molecular energy levels arising from the interantid…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205128] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Magnetically tunable quantum Hall triple-antidot molecules realized in suspended graphene
item.dc:creator: Naomi Mizuno, Dmitri V. Averin, Xu Du
